creator: josh@tcbug.org
text:
PF appears to mangle packets on the loopback, even with pass all rules

Fix: 

set skip on lo0 fixes the problem, unfortunately I need that too.
How-To-Repeat: 

simple pf.conf

pass in all keep state
pass out all keep state

root@homebase /home/jpaetzel ->cat echoserver.py
#!/usr/bin/env python

"""
A simple echo server
"""

import socket

host = ''
port = 50000
backlog = 5
size = 1024
s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
s.bind((host,port))
s.listen(backlog)
while 1:
    client, address = s.accept()
    data = client.recv(size)
    if data:
        client.send(data)
    client.close()

*********************************************8

root@homebase /home/jpaetzel ->cat echoclient.py
#!/usr/bin/env python

"""
A simple echo client
"""

import socket , time

host = '127.0.0.2'
port = 50000
size = 1024
loop = 1
while 1:
    loop += 1
    s = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    s.connect((host,port))
    s.send('Hello, world')
    data = s.recv(size)
    s.close()
    print '#', loop, 'received:', data, time.ctime()

Bring up echoserver.py on 127.0.0.2 (in a jail works), start
echoclient.py on another loopback IP in the host environment or another
jail and watch it go boom.  Disabling pf or set skip on lo0 lets it run
indefinitely.
